description:
The Middle Length Lam-Rim (Lam rim 'bring ba in Tibetan) is one of three texts on the "Stages of the Path to Enlightenment" composed by Lama Tsongkhapa (1357–1419), the great Tibetan Buddhist master and founder of the Gelug school. The three versions — Great, Middle Length, and Small — are arranged by scope and depth, and this middle one strikes a balance between the comprehensive elaboration of the Great Lam-Rim and the more condensed Small version.

description:
Subject: Special Teaching: The Vajra Cutter Sutra | Ven. Tenzin Namjong | Sessions 3 & 4, July 2026

Dear Friends,

We are delighted to invite you to the concluding sessions of our special teaching series on one of the most profound texts in the Mahāyāna Buddhist tradition.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SPECIAL TEACHING
The Vajra Cutter Sutra
with Venerable Tenzin Namjong
At Choe Khor Sum Ling, Bengaluru
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Free and open to all. No registration required.

📅 SESSION DATES & TIMES

Session 3 — Saturday, 4 July 2026 | 5:00 pm – 7:00 pm
Session 4 — Sunday, 5 July 2026 | 10:00 am – 12:30 pm

🔗 https://us02web.zoom.us/j/89800597231?pwd=SBVXzqD61bJIWXoTdW5oT6tbVUbJSx.1
Meeting ID: 898 0059 7231 | Passcode: 226737

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ABOUT THE VAJRA CUTTER SUTRA

The Vajra Cutter Sutra — known in Sanskrit as the Vajracchedikā Prajñāpāramitā Sūtra, or the Diamond Sutra — is one of the most celebrated texts of the Mahāyāna tradition. Presented as a dialogue between the Buddha and his disciple Subhuti, it goes to the very heart of the teachings on emptiness and the nature of reality.

The title itself is illuminating: vajra means diamond or thunderbolt — something that cuts through all things without itself being cut. The sutra's wisdom is like that: sharp, penetrating, and liberating.

Its teachings directly address the mind that clings — to a self, to outcomes, to ideas of spiritual achievement — and in dissolving that grasping, open the heart to compassion in ways that transform daily life.

For those joining us for the first time, these sessions offer a guided and accessible entry into the sutra. For those who attended Sessions 1 and 2 in June, Venerable Namjong's continuing commentary will open fresh dimensions of meaning. Simply encountering the Vajra Cutter is considered deeply fortunate.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ABOUT THE TEACHER

Venerable Tenzin Namjong was born and raised in Hawaii and followed a wide and sincere spiritual path before finding his home in the Tibetan Buddhist tradition — drawn there through the kindness of His Holiness the Dalai Lama and Lama Zopa Rinpoche.

His earlier years of study in both Zen and Theravada traditions give him an unusually broad perspective on Buddhist practice and philosophy. He completed 17 years of the traditional Geshe Studies Programme at Sera Je Monastery in South India, and holds a Bachelor of Arts in Philosophy from Princeton University.

Following the advice of Lama Zopa Rinpoche, he now dedicates himself primarily to meditation retreat and practice. Venerable Namjong is a registered FPMT teacher and a beloved regular at Choe Khor Sum Ling.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This teaching is free and open to everyone.
No registration required.
Simply come with an open mind and an open heart.

Warm wishes,
The Team at Choe Khor Sum Ling

description:
🙏 His Holiness the 14th Dalai Lama's 91st Birthday Celebrations
Choe Khor Sum Ling (CKSL), Bengaluru

Dear friends,

This July, we have the joy of celebrating the 91st birthday of His Holiness the Dalai Lama — a life that has been, for so many of us, a living refuge of compassion and wisdom. We warmly invite you to join us for two special days at the centre.

Monday, 6 July 2026 | 5:00 – 8:00 pm

Birthday Celebrations for His Holiness the Dalai Lama
Led by Venerable Tenzin Namjong

  • Vajra Cutter Sutra Recitation
  • Alokhe — Extensive Light Offerings
  • Long Life Prayers for His Holiness

✨ Sponsor a Light
This Alokhe offering is an opportunity for each of us to light something — for His Holiness, and for all beings. You're warmly invited to sponsor:


SponsorshipAmount
Full Sponsorship₹25,000
Individual — Tea Lamp₹100 per lamp
Individual — Electric Lamp₹450 per lamp

Both programmes are offered freely. We gratefully welcome dana in support of CKSL and its activities. Dana may be offered at the centre or via bank transfer (bank details available at the venue).

To sponsor a light or for any questions, do reach out to us.

Everyone is warmly welcome.

May this birthday be one of long life, good health, and continued teachings for His Holiness, and may the merit of this gathering ripple out to all beings.

With folded hands,
Choe Khor Sum Ling (CKSL), Bengaluru
